intro: Converting JavaScript tasks to Bash moves code from an application runtime environment straight to the operating system shell interface. Developers port to Bash to remove dependencies like Node.js for low-overhead deployment scripts, systems automation, and direct pipeline manipulations of command-line tools. This eliminates heavy node_modules folders in favor of lean, process-centric automation.

diffs.from: Dynamic types supporting strings, numbers, booleans, and nested complex object trees.
diffs.to: Untyped string-centric architecture where variables are treated as strings unless evaluated in arithmetic contexts.

diffs.from: Structured C-style code with blocks demarcated by curly braces and keyword statements.
diffs.to: Command-line syntax where whitespace determines argument parsing and conditionals end with reversed keywords (e.g., fi, esac).

diffs.from: V8 heap managed by a garbage collector with closures maintaining scope state.
diffs.to: No centralized heap; uses process isolation and short-lived subshells that clean up on exit.

diffs.from: Asynchronous engine processing macro-tasks and micro-tasks via an event loop.
diffs.to: Synchronous by default, utilizing background workers via the `&` operator and process pipelines.

diffs.from: EcmaScript APIs supplemented by Node.js or browser modules for external system interaction.
diffs.to: Relies entirely on binary executables found in the system PATH (sed, awk, grep, curl) and streams.

examples.title: Conditional Directory Check
examples.description: Checking if a directory exists before executing an operation.

examples.title: Looping Through Command Output
examples.description: Iterating over text records separated by lines.
examples.toCode:
files=("app.js" "index.js")
for file in "${files[@]}"; do
  echo "Processing: ${file}"
done

tips.title: Quote Your Variables Judiciously
tips.body: Unlike JavaScript string variables, referencing a Bash variable without double quotes (e.g., `$var`) will trigger word splitting and glob expansion if the content contains spaces. Always wrap references in quotes: `"$var"`.

tips.title: Check Command Return Values
tips.body: Bash does not throw exceptions. Error handling requires inspecting the exit status code of the preceding command using `$?`; a non-zero value indicates an error state.

tips.title: Mind the Whitespace in Conditionals
tips.body: In Bash, brackets inside `if [ "$a" == "$b" ]` are actual commands, not merely syntax boundaries. Missing spaces between the brackets and arguments will cause fatal parser syntax errors.

tips.title: Enable Strict Mode via Flags
tips.body: Emulate JavaScript's 'use strict' by adding `set -euo pipefail` at the start of your Bash scripts. This instructs the shell to exit immediately if any command fails or if an uninitialized variable is used.
